blockingIssues.description: `RestrictedFS.lstat()` still delegates directly to `this.vfs.lstat(path)` after only a lexical `isAllowed()` check, so when `lstatSync()` returns null for an ancestor symlink, `VfsAdapter.lstat()` can fall back to an async path that still follows the ancestor symlink out of sandbox and leak metadata through shell `lstat` consumers. Because that adapter surface remains ACL-unsafe, `fix-restricted-fs-shell-contract` is not yet fully re-verified.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to record that bridge-scoop cleanup now removes registry state before the terminal `onStatusChange('ready')` callback, closing the ghost-row gap verified by `fix-agent-scoop-cleanup-ordering`.

Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to note that the `readDirSync()` symlink-directory gap is fixed and that the remaining RestrictedFS ACL caveat is the async `lstat()` fallback on ancestor symlinks discovered while reviewing `fix-restricted-fs-readdirsync-symlink-escape`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the precedence for required manual CDP smoke checks when port 5710 is already occupied: mission guidance should explicitly say whether workers must kill the existing occupant and continue, or return immediately, so UI-visible fixes do not skip meaningful browser verification.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both visibility-related workers skipped a meaningful port-5710 smoke run because mission AGENTS.md told them to try killing the occupant first while the feature-level instructions told them to stop and return if the port was already held. That ambiguity produced two handoffs without real browser validation of the updated bundle.
